title: iPhone X pricing controversy: $999 price point and consumer acceptance

ground_truth_outcome.source: https://www.macrumors.com/2018/04/23/iphone-x-siri-survey-creative-strategies/ — Creative Strategies survey (N=1746 iPhone X owners): 97% satisfaction, 85% 'very satisfied'; Piper Jaffray: 31% of non-upgraders cited price (69% neutral-positive)

polling_trajectory.source: https://9to5mac.com/2017/08/24/iphone-loyalty/ — Fluent survey (N=2117): 67% said $999 is 'too expensive', only 12% said rightly priced; Barclays survey: only 18% of iPhone owners willing to pay >$1000

https://appleinsider.com/articles/17/10/23/survey-finds-iphone-x-8-purchase-intent-at-highest-level-since-apple-launched-the-iphone-6 — 69% of North American consumers planning to buy new iPhone within 3 months; highest since iPhone 6

https://www.macrumors.com/2017/11/06/iphone-x-adoption-localytics/ — Localytics: iPhone X captured 0.93% of total iPhone share in first weekend (highest since iPhone 5s vs. 0.3% for iPhone 8); pre-orders sold out in <10 minutes

Strategy Analytics: camera #1 driver of positive sentiment, display and battery ~80% positive (https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/iphone-xs-camera-drives-consumer-sentiment-finds-strategy-analytics-300573259.html); The Verge 9/10 review

https://www.macrumors.com/2018/03/05/iphone-x-no-upgrade-survey/ — Piper Jaffray (N=1500 iPhone owners): 31% cited 'too expensive' as reason not to upgrade (inverse: 69% did not find price prohibitive); CIRP: iPhone X ~20% of Q4 sales, top seller every week

events.description: Apple announces iPhone X at $999 — first mainstream smartphone to break $1000; immediate media sticker shock
iPhone 8/8 Plus launch — lukewarm reception, below-trend upgrade rates. Market interprets as 'everyone waiting for X'
iPhone X pre-orders open and sell out within minutes; 5-6 week shipping delays across all carriers
iPhone X launch day: lines at Apple Stores worldwide; first reviews praise Face ID and OLED display
Analyst reports indicate iPhone X drove Apple to record Q1 FY2018 revenue ($88.3B); ASP highest ever at $796

agents.name: Tim Cook
agents.description: Apple CEO, framed $999 as 'value of the future'

agents.name: Phil Schiller
agents.description: SVP Marketing, presented iPhone X at Keynote

agents.name: Marques Brownlee (MKBHD)
agents.description: Top tech YouTuber; initially cautious, then positive review

agents.name: Wall Street analysts
agents.description: Mixed: supply concerns vs. ASP uplift optimism

agents.name: Consumer Reports
agents.description: Independent reviewer; eventually recommended iPhone X

agents.name: Tech media (The Verge, Wired)
agents.description: Initial sticker shock coverage shifted to positive reviews post-launch

agents.name: Apple loyalists / early adopters
agents.description: Would buy regardless; drove pre-order sellout

agents.name: Budget-conscious consumers
agents.description: Sticker shock: $999 seen as excessive; considered Android alternatives

agents.name: Android users / skeptics
agents.description: Mocked notch design and price; Samsung Galaxy S8 comparisons

notes: Pro = positive about iPhone X / willing to buy at $999, Against = too expensive / negative reaction. This is a classic commercial backlash-to-acceptance arc. Initial sticker shock (round 1) was overcome by supply scarcity signaling, overwhelmingly positive reviews, and Apple's brand power. Rounds 2-4 polling data are estimates from analyst reports and media sentiment; rounds 1 and 5 have survey citations. AAPL stock rose ~18% from Sep to Dec 2017.
